items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant economic activity in the area. This is the only non-residential project in the recent permit batch.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is rolling out an amnesty program to help businesses get compliant with licensing requirements, coupled with outreach to shopping centers. This could affect local business owners who may have overlooked renewal deadlines or registration.

items.headline: New economic incentives for retail and small businesses announced
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing incentive programs to attract and support retail, small businesses, and startups in the city. This signals the council's focus on diversifying the local economy beyond tech companies.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: The City Council approved making the Great American Litter Pick-up an annual city-sponsored special event with city funding. The city is also authorized to accept donations of materials and services to support the cleanup effort.

items.headline: Community Development Funding Opportunities for Local Projects
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will review and present applicants seeking federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These grants fund housing and community development projects that directly benefit Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area planning study to examine land use and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying potential changes to land use and transportation in the San Antonio Road area. This study session could lead to future zoning or development decisions affecting that neighborhood.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies being written into zoning code
items.summary: The Commission is considering amendments to Title 16 and Title 18 of municipal code to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. These changes could affect how retail spaces are zoned, designed, or developed throughout Palo Alto.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, allowing residents to keep bees in Campbell with new regulations. The changes reflect evolving city policy on urban agriculture and pollinator support.
